Dull Bird
HomeEssaysIn PracticeAbout 中文

About

I named this place Dull Bird. There’s a Chinese saying — the dull bird flies early. Mine also flies slow. The instant, noisy, frequency-driven way of speaking isn’t my rhythm. I’m not good at social media, but I’ve always wanted to say something to the world. So here it is.

Two kinds of things live here: essays and thoughts. Essays are honest notes about my life and myself; thoughts are me trying to think something blurry into focus. I want them to have a little depth — not to seem clever, but because only writing that slows down is worth reading slowly.

One belief

The future is already here — it’s just not evenly distributed.

— William Gibson

I keep this line close. The most advanced technology, the best tools, the widest views — they already exist, in some corners of the world. Most people just haven’t had the chance to reach them. My wish is small, and also large: to make the future that has already arrived a little more equal for everyone.

I can’t do much. But I can take what I see, what I learn, what I build, and explain it as plainly as I can — to people who haven’t had the chance yet. If it spares even one person a detour, it was worth it. That’s why the “In Practice” section exists: to turn abstract technology into plain words.

Here, “equal” isn’t just a slogan. It hides in concrete places: pages kept simple, loading kept fast, contrast and type sized for people who find reading hard, full keyboard support, and an RSS feed so you can follow along without depending on any platform. Making content reach more people is itself a kind of equality.

Also for myself

Writing is how I sort myself out. Only when the tangled thoughts become words can I see what I actually think. So half of this is spoken to the world, half to myself. I hope that while making the world a little better, I get a little better too.

The dull bird is in no hurry. One step at a time.

Contact: GitHub @dull-bird

In dialogue with the world, through depth · evening out the future, a little
